symbols: guitar — instrument of praise and the musical tradition of African American gospel worship
open mouth in song — vocal praise as embodied prayer and spiritual expression
stained-glass-like background — the sacred context of gospel music rooted in the church
two figures in close harmony — communal worship and the unity of voices lifted to God
description: Two African American figures are shown in close-up, deeply absorbed in musical performance. In the foreground, a figure in a red garment plays a guitar, head tilted back with mouth open in song. Behind, a second figure with an open, impassioned expression sings with full voice. The background is composed of angular geometric shapes in blues, greens, yellows, and warm tones reminiscent of stained glass. The figures are rendered with strong, rounded forms and rich, warm skin tones. The overall composition conveys intense energy and devotion.

scripture_refs.reason: Praise Him with stringed instruments — the biblical mandate for musical worship that gospel music fulfills
Singing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs with gratitude in your hearts to God
